what_it_does: Renders a top-left brand bug with a logo and a hub switcher (tabs) that lets the user pick among available hubs.
observations: Functional React component named HubBug with props hubs, activeHub, onSwitch.
Renders a logo image from a relative path ../../assets/logos/conceptv-v-yellow.png with alt text ConceptV.
Displays a hardcoded STAGE label above the tabs.
Maps hubs into buttons, applying an active class when h.id equals activeHub.
Assigns the component to window.HubBug at the end of the file.
standouts: Hardcoded relative logo path and hardcoded STAGE label.
Component is exposed globally via window.HubBug.
No prop validation or TypeScript types.
conventions: Uses a functional component with destructured props.
Uses className strings and conditional active class.
Provides a key prop when mapping arrays.
concerns: Relative image path may break depending on build/deployment structure.
Global window assignment couples the component to a browser runtime.
STAGE label is not configurable via props.
No fallback if hubs is empty or undefined.
notes: A small brand-plus-switcher header piece that exposes itself globally and bakes in a logo path and stage label.
questions: Is window.HubBug required for a specific legacy loading mechanism?
Should STAGE be a prop rather than a hardcoded label?
How is the relative logo path resolved in the final build?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a brand bug and hub switcher.
intention_signals: Provide a persistent, recognizable header control that lets users switch between different hubs or environments while keeping the brand visible.
contribution: A compact hub-switching UI component with an active-tab indicator and a brand logo slot, suitable for reuse as a top-left navigation control.
summary_for_embedding: HubBug.jsx is a React functional component that renders a branded top-left bug with a tabbed hub switcher driven by hubs, activeHub, and onSwitch props.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a reusable brand-and-hub-switcher control for a virtual-hub interface.

symbols.does: Renders a brand logo and a tabbed hub switcher.
symbols.description: HubBug is a functional React component that receives a list of hubs, the currently active hub id, and an onSwitch callback. It renders a logo image and a row of tab buttons, highlighting the active hub and invoking onSwitch with the selected hub id when clicked.
